title: Sandalwood and rose cold-process soap
subtitle: A warm, floral bar coloured with rose kaolin clay and scented with sandalwood and rose geranium essential oils
excerpt: Sandalwood essential oil and rose geranium combine in a warm, floral-woody fragrance that suits both unscented and lightly fragranced skin care. Rose kaolin clay gives this cold-process bar a dusty pink tone and a silky lather. Built on an olive, coconut, shea, and castor base at 5% superfat, the bar is mild and conditioning.
sourceNotes: SAP values: olive oil 0.134, coconut oil 0.190, shea butter 0.128, castor oil 0.128. 5% superfat; 33% lye discount. Reference: Susan Miller Cavitch, The Soapmaker's Companion (1997). Rose kaolin as a soap colourant: Bramble Berry natural colourant guide (2023). Sandalwood EO (Santalum album) in cold-process soap: behaves well with no acceleration; safe at 3% of oil weight. Rose geranium EO (Pelargonium graveolens): stable in CP soap; blends well with sandalwood.

recipe.batchNotes: Recalculate lye weight for any oil changes. The clay and essential oils are additives; they do not affect the lye calculation.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Allow 2 days in the mould before unmoulding. Minimum 4-week cure; 6 weeks for best results.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Two years in a cool, dark place on an open rack. Discard any bar with orange oily spots (DOS) or a paint-like smell.

glossaryTerms.definition: The point at which the soap batter has thickened to a custard-like consistency. The moment to add clay and fragrance.
The percentage of oil left unsaponified in the finished bar. At 5%, the bar is conditioning and mild.
The reaction between fat and lye that produces soap and glycerine.

projectSchedule.title: Unmould and cut
projectSchedule.body: Unmould after 2 days. The bar will be a soft pink. Cut wearing protective gloves.

projectSchedule.title: Four-week check
projectSchedule.body: Test a sliver on skin. Ready to use. The pink will have lightened to a pale blush.

projectSchedule.title: Cure complete
projectSchedule.body: Fully cured. Hard, mild, and stable for two years.

content.text: Sandalwood essential oil and rose geranium essential oil together produce a warm, floral-woody scent. Rose kaolin clay gives this bar a soft pink colour and a silky surface on use. The oil blend of 50% olive, 30% coconut, 15% shea, and 5% castor gives a conditioning, lathering bar. At 5%
superfat
and 33% lye discount, it is mild enough for most skin types. The clay is dispersed in oil before addition at
trace
to prevent clumping. Yield is 8 bars of around 120 g each.
Put on protective gloves and goggles before starting.
Ingredients

items.prepNote: 30% of oil weight; melt if solid
Dispersed in 2 tablespoons of the weighed olive oil

content.text: Method
Prepare the clay blend
Mix the rose kaolin clay into 2 tablespoons of the weighed olive oil until smooth. Set aside.
Mix the lye solution

In a ventilated space with goggles and protective gloves on, weigh the lye separately. Pour distilled water into the HDPE pitcher. Add lye to water in a steady stream, stirring with a stainless-steel spoon. Never add water to lye. Cool to 38 to 42°C.
Combine and blend to trace

Melt the coconut oil and shea butter together. Combine all oils in a heat-safe bowl. Cool to 38 to 42°C. Pour the lye solution into the oils. Stick-blend to light trace. Stir in the clay-oil mixture and both essential oils. Blend for 5 seconds. Pour into the mould. The
saponification
reaction generates heat over the next 24 to 48 hours. Wrap in a towel and leave undisturbed.
Unmould and cure
Unmould after 2 days. Cut into bars wearing protective gloves. Cure on an open rack for 4 to 6 weeks in a cool, dry room.
Storage and shelf life
Two years on an open rack in a cool, dark place. Discard on signs of DOS or a paint-like smell.
